TaDa! Presentation mode for HTML

Product deck

Present any HTML like a deck.

Give TaDa! a file, URL, localhost page, or pasted HTML. It turns generated HTML into a clean client-ready presentation.

Open HTML Load URL Present fullscreen
01 / 12

The shift

AI writes HTML. Teams need to present it.

Claude, Codex, Gemini, and ChatGPT can produce reports, demos, mockups, one-page pitches, and interactive mini-apps. The output is often impressive. The presentation moment is not.

01

Client demos

Generated HTML becomes the fastest way to show a proposal, prototype, or product concept.

02

Internal reviews

Teams use AI-made HTML to review strategy, research, UI ideas, and status reports.

03

Local experiments

Many demos run from a local file or localhost, not a polished hosted website.

04

Fast iteration

The work changes quickly. The presenter needs to open, test, and show it without ceremony.

02 / 12

The problem

Browsers are not presentation mode.

tab

Browser chrome

Tabs, address bars, bookmarks, and resizing pull attention away from the work.

fit

Wrong framing

HTML pages scroll, overflow, or start halfway through a layout when you need a slide.

?

Unclear boundaries

AI output may use sections, divs, headings, comments, or a single scripted canvas.

js

Fragile local assets

Generated files often depend on nearby scripts and assets that break at the worst moment.

The opportunity: make generated HTML feel as easy to present as a PowerPoint deck.

03 / 12

The product

TaDa! is the bridge from HTML to showtime.

It focuses on one job: load an HTML artifact, understand its slide structure, and present it cleanly.

1

Load

Open a file, paste HTML, drop a deck, or enter a URL.

2

Normalize

Detect slides from common AI and web patterns.

3

Review

Use the side rail, thumbnails, and presenter notes.

4

Present

Go fullscreen, move forward/back, and exit with Escape.

04 / 12

Any source

File, URL, localhost, or paste.

.html

Open a local HTML file from Finder or the app.

file://

Load a direct local file URL when an AI gives you a path.

localhost

Present a local dev server without deploying first.

URL

Use remote HTTP or HTTPS pages when the file is hosted.

paste

Paste raw HTML directly when saving a file is too much friction.

Built for the handoff from AI to presentation.

Drop the artifact in, skim the slide rail, and start presenting.

deck.html
05 / 12

Structure aware

It understands how AI tends to make decks.

TaDa! looks for common slide signals first, then falls back gracefully when the document is less structured.

<section> and custom selectors
.deck structures, section tags, and custom selectors
<hr> and <!-- slide -->
h1/h2 heading fallback
single scripted document when needed
<section>
  <h1>Client-ready HTML</h1>
  <p>A complete slide in one file.</p>
</section>
<section> ... </section>
06 / 12

Sample: Ely

The Ely demo becomes a client-ready show.

The original HTML deck includes styled slides, thumbnails, local structure, and visual effects. TaDa! keeps the deck intact while adding presentation controls.

10 slides detected side rail ready halftone art visible
07 / 12

Presentation controls

PowerPoint-style movement for HTML.

08 / 12

Snappy by design

Slide navigation should feel instant.

0.6ms

Average UI-visible navigation

Measured in the packaged app while clicking through the Ely deck.
1.9ms

Max measured update

No more multi-second spikes from rebuilding every thumbnail iframe.
68

Tests passing

Coverage includes ingest, deck detection, security, visual system, and production config.

The big change: moving forward/back updates the live slide and active thumbnail instead of re-rendering the whole deck rail.

09 / 12

Standalone shows

Export an HTML show that opens ready to present.

TaDa! can export a show file that starts directly in presentation mode. Send it to someone and they can click through the deck without learning the app UI first.

TaDa! show

Fullscreen by default, keyboard navigation built in, and a clean escape path back out.

show.html
10 / 12

Product shape

A focused desktop tool for modern presentation work.

Mac

macOS first

Apple Silicon package is already built, with a Mac-native hidden-titlebar feel.

Win

Windows path

Electron packaging is configured for Windows NSIS builds and shared app icons.

Up

Auto-update ready

Release metadata and update plumbing are wired for GitHub Releases.

The product boundary stays simple: TaDa! displays and presents HTML. It does not edit slides, generate content, or replace a design tool.

11 / 12

The promise

Drop HTML. Cue TaDa!

From AI-generated artifact to polished client presentation in one focused desktop app.
